rationale: The cited paper is used beyond a passing mention: it is referenced in the theorizing around firm business scope and appears as a source for specific variable operationalizations in the controls table. The citing paper also explicitly positions its managerial implication against Groening et al. (2016), noting prior marketing-HR coordination evidence and then extending coordination logic to marketing with sales/finance/accounting via advertising and receivables. This indicates a meaningful conceptual and positioning role for the cited work in the citing paper's setup.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the cited paper's cross-functional valuation logic by moving from customer-employee signal consistency to the joint effects of advertising and receivables on shareholder value. It also carries forward related scope-oriented framing and measurement conventions while testing a new pair of strategic activities.
